Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Olympics: When Team SA is in action on Saturday

Team SA will have an opportunity to add to their Olympics medal haul on Saturday as the Paris Games draw towards a close.

Team SA are involved in five sports at the 2024 Paris Olympics on Saturday, witha few medal opportunities up for grabs.

There are three further track and field medals to fight for in the evening session, with Brian Raats (high jump), Jo-Ane van Dyk (javelin) and the Team SA men’s 4x400m relay all in action. Van Dyk and the men’s 4x400m are considered realistic medal contenders.

Team SA are currently43rd on the overall medal table, with one gold (Tatjana Smith), two silvers (Smith and men’s 4x100m) and two bronzes (men’s Sevens rugby and Alan Hatherly).

ATHLETICS

Men’s high jump, final (7pm): Brian Raats

Women’s javelin, final (7.30pm): Jo-Ane van Dyk

Men’s 4x400m, final (9pm): Team SA

CANOEING

Men’s kayak single 1000m semi-finals (11.20am):Hamish Lovemore

CYCLING

Men’s keiren, first round heats (5.31pm):Jean Spies

GOLF

Women’s individual, fourth round (12.06pm):Ashleigh Buhai is on 141 after three rounds. She’s shot 68, 73 and 74 for 215.

WRESTLING

Men’s 97kg freestyle, 1/8 final:Nicholas de Lange vs Givi Matcharashvili (Georgia)

Compiled by GARY LEMKE in Paris

ATHLETICS

Men’s 4x400m relay, heats:Team SA finished 8th in their heat in 3:03.19 and didn’t qualify for the final after Antonie Nortje suffered a mishap on the third leg when he fell at the changeover. However, after the race Nigeria were disqualified and SA were promoted into the final by the referee.

Men’s 800m semi-finals:Edmund du Plessis finished 4th in his semi-final in 1:45.34. This left him outside the automatic two qualifiers from the heat, and outside of being a fastest loser.

Men’s 4x100m relay, final:SILVER!Team SA finished second behind Canada in an African record 37.57

CANOEING

Men’s double kayak 500m semi-finals:Hamish Lovemore and Andrew Birkett finished 6th in 1:29.70. The top 4 boats went through, so the Team SA duo qualified for the B-final

Men’s double kayak 500m B-final:Lovemore and Birkett finished 4th in 1:31.79. That means they ended their campaign in 12th position

DIVING

Women’s 3m springboard final:Julia Vincent finished 11th in the final, with a total score of 271.25 points. She had risen to as high as third after three of the five dives.

IN PROGRESS

GOLF

Women’s individual, third round

-8Lydia Ko (NZ)

-7Zhang Rose (USA), Morgane Metraux (Swit)

-6Mariaju Uribe (Col), Miyu Yamashita (Japan), Atthaya Thitikul (Thai)

Team SA golfers

-1 (T16th)Ashleigh Buhai (68, 73, 74, 215)

+11(T49th)Paula Reto (78, 73, 76, 227)
